name: Posole
recipeInstructions:
This dish is a traditional New Year's Day stew. It is thick with hominy, beans and pork.
1. Cut the pork into 1/2-inch cubes.
2. Heat oil and garlic in a 3-quart saucepan until the oil is hot. Coat the pork with flour. Cook and stir the pork in the oil over medium heat until brown; remove the pork with a slotted spoon and drain.
3. Cook and stir 1/2 cup onion in the same saucepan until tender. Stir in beans, hominy, carrot, celery, green chiles, ground red chiles, and broth. Heat to boiling; reduce heat.
4. Cover and simmer for 10 minutes. Stir pork, salt, and pepper into vegetable mixture. Heat to boiling; reduce heat.
5. Cover and simmer for 30 minutes. Sprinkle with oregano, 1/4 cup of onion, and cilantro. Serve with lime wedges and tortilla chips.
